title: Polestar Q1 2026 Revenue Flat as Net Loss Widens
text: Polestar reported flat Q1 2026 revenue of $633 million despite volume growth, as the company faced continued pressure from tariffs and intense competition. Net loss widened significantly to $383 million, up 131% year-over-year, overshadowing volume improvements. Retail sales volume grew 7% to 13,126 units, but pricing pressure and tariff impacts prevented conversion to bottom-line profitability. The company's gross margin turned sharply negative at -3.2%, deteriorating from Q1 2025 levels. Management attributed the margin pressure to increased tariffs on imported automotive parts, competitive pricing dynamics, and currency headwinds. Despite operational challenges, the company maintains strategic progress with 230 retail sales points operational and plans to reach 250 by year-end 2026. Cash position remains stable at $676 million following facility renewals. The company expects sequential revenue growth throughout 2026, with Q4 projected at $1.2 billion. Management emphasized upcoming product launches including the Polestar 5 and new Polestar 4 variants as critical for profitability and market competitiveness.
